name: Thrud
description: Thrud is a Norse goddess, the daughter of Thor, the god of thunder, and Sif, a goddess associated with earth and fertility. Her name translates to "strength" or "power," reflecting her inherent nature and lineage. While not as prominently featured as some other Norse deities, Thrud embodies the qualities of strength, both physical and perhaps metaphorical, associated with the Aesir gods. She is sometimes mentioned in contexts relating to potential betrothals, though these stories are often fragmented and incomplete. In one tale, the dwarf Alviss seeks to marry her, believing her beauty and lineage make her a worthy bride. However, Thor, ever protective of his daughter, delays Alviss until sunrise, at which point the dwarf turns to stone, a testament to Thor's cunning and Thrud's value. Although her role in the broader Norse mythology is relatively minor, Thrud's existence reinforces the themes of strength, family bonds, and the protective nature of the gods. Some speculate she may have been associated with Valkyries, further solidifying her connection to strength and battle. Ultimately, Thrud represents a powerful, though somewhat enigmatic, figure within the Norse pantheon, her potential and full story remaining a subject of ongoing interpretation and speculation.
subtitle: Daughter of Thor, Goddess of Strength

keyMyths.title: Alviss's Pursuit
keyMyths.description: The dwarf Alviss sought to marry Thrud, but Thor tricked him into staying until sunrise, turning him to stone.

keyMyths.title: Daughter of Thor
keyMyths.description: Thrud is known primarily as the daughter of Thor and Sif, inheriting strength from her father.

keyMyths.title: Potential Valkyrie Association
keyMyths.description: Some theories suggest a connection to the Valkyries, further linking her to strength and battle, though evidence is limited.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source of Norse mythology.
sources.text: A key source of Norse mythology.

sources.author: John Lindow
sources.work: Norse Mythology: A Guide to the Gods, Heroes, Rituals, and Beliefs
